title: Klasea grandifolia
taxonomy: Klasea grandifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It belongs to the genus Klasea, which is a group of plants that are native to various parts of the world. The family Boraginaceae consists of a diverse group of plants that are commonly known as borage or forget-me-nots. Klasea grandifolia is classified as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Boraginales, Family: Boraginaceae, Genus: Klasea, Species: K. grandifolia. This classification helps to identify the plant's relationships with other species and its place within the plant kingdom.

intro: Klasea grandifolia, also known as Himalayan Klasea, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Boraginaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Klasea grandifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
